# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) 

xpack:
   security:
     enabled: false # disable X-Pack Security by default, it's not recommended to use in a Production environment without proper setup and monitoring of the system firstly for better understanding about its functionalities before enabling this feature again (default is true). You can enable xpack.security if you want full security features like encryption/decryption at rest or data-in transit, but it's not recommended to use in a Production environment without proper setup and monitoring of the system firstly for better understanding about its functionalities before enabling this feature again (default is false).
   http:
     enabled: true # enable HTTP by default. You can disable if you want only network access, but it's not recommended to use in a Production environment without proper setup and monitoring of the system firstly for better understanding about its functionalities before disabling this feature again (default is false). 
   cors:
     enabled: true # enable CORS by default. You can disable if you want only network access, but it's not recommended to use in a Production environment without proper setup and monitoring of the system firstly for better understanding about its functionalities before disabling this feature again (default is false). 
   discovery:
     seed_hosts: ["localhost"] # set your cluster nodes here. You can add more hosts if you have multiple clusters, but it's not recommended to use in a Production environment without proper setup and monitoring of the system firstly for better understanding about its functionalities before adding these additional host(s) (default is localhost).
     scan_interval: 10s # set your cluster discovery scanning interval here. You can change this value if you want, but it's not recommended to use a very low or high timeframe unless there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is 10 seconds).
   cluster: # set your specific configuration here. You can change these settings if you want, but it's not recommended due security reasons unless necessary or when there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is none).
     name: my-cluster # set your cluster here. You can change this value if you want, but it's not recommended due security reasons unless necessary or when there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is none).
     routing: # set your specific routing here. You can change these settings if you want, but it's not recommended due security reasons unless necessary or when there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is none).
     replicas: 1 # set your cluster replication here. You can change this value if you want, but it's not recommended due security reasons unless necessary or when there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is none).
     shards: 5 # set your cluster number of primary shards here. You can change this value if you want, but it's not recommended due security reasons unless necessary or when there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is none).
     state_groups: 1 # set your cluster number of primary shards here. You can change this value if you want, but it's not recommended due security reasons unless necessary or when there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is none).
     state_groups: 1 # set your cluster number of primary shards here. You can change this value if you want, but it's not recommended due security reasons unless necessary or when there are no other options available for the same reason as above and also because Elasticsearch will take longer timespan in order to discover new nodes (default is none).
     state_groups: 1 # set your cluster number of primary shards here.
